---
title: "Choose Which Version of a Program Runs by Default with `update-alternatives`"
subtitle: "Note#200"
id: "200"
date: 2026-03-10
date-modified: ""
date-format: iso
categories: [notes]
spotlight: ""
---
Debian's `update-alternatives` manages symlinks in `/etc/alternatives/` so that several programs providing the same functionality (e.g., `php`, `java`) can coexist, and you pick which one the generic name points to.

## Register an Alternative

```bash
update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/php php /usr/bin/php8.2 82
update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/php php /usr/bin/php8.3 83
```

The last number is the priority. Highest priority wins in auto mode.

## Switch

```bash
# Interactive picker
update-alternatives --config php

# Or set it directly
update-alternatives --set java /usr/lib/jvm/java-21-openjdk-amd64/bin/java
```

## Inspect

```bash
# Show current choice and all registered alternatives
update-alternatives --display php

# List all managed groups
update-alternatives --get-selections
```

## Remove

```bash
update-alternatives --remove php /usr/bin/php8.2
```

## References

* `man update-alternatives`
